message:
fix(reactor): io_uring ETIME handling, Windows modify stale socket cleanup

io_uring.rs:
- Handle ETIME from submit_with_args as a non-error condition. When io_uring
  reports timeout expiry via ETIME, this is expected behavior for reactor poll
  semantics (the timeout elapsed with no events) — not an operational failure.
  Previously, ETIME was propagated as an error causing callers to treat a
  normal timeout as a reactor malfunction
- Add test that polls with a short timeout on a socket with no pending data,
  verifying zero events are returned without error

windows.rs:
- Harden modify() to detect and prune stale registrations when the underlying
  socket has been closed. When poller.modify() returns an "already gone" error
  (ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE, ERROR_NOT_FOUND, or WSAENOTSOCK), remove the stale
  token from bookkeeping and return NotFound. This matches the epoll and kqueue
  reactors' behavior where modify on a closed fd cleans up stale state instead
  of leaving orphaned registrations that block fd/socket-number reuse
- Re-lookup info via get_mut after successful modify to update interest, since
  the original borrow was consumed by the error check path
- Add test that registers a socket, drops it, attempts modify, and verifies
  the stale registration is pruned (registration_count drops to 0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
